hyperedges.label: CyberMilo Planning and Roadmap Document Chain

nodes.label: CyberMilo Project Audit and Market Gap
nodes.norm_label: cybermilo project audit and market gap

nodes.label: CyberMilo EduOS (Education ERP Product)
nodes.rationale: Positioned as 'One AI Platform for Every Institution' (school, college, university, coaching). Differentiators chosen: AI copilot per role, Student Digital Twin, Smart Campus Command Center, glassmorphism UI, multi-tenant SaaS — depth of AI features is the wedge against baseline ERP competitors identified in the market-gap audit.

nodes.label: CyberMilo TravelOS (AI Tourism Intelligence OS)
nodes.rationale: Explicitly must NOT be built or marketed as a normal travel CRM; positioned as an AI Tourism Intelligence Operating System built first for Jammu & Kashmir tourism (Gulmarg, Amarnath, Dal Lake) with region-specific intelligence, then expandable globally. Becomes 'real' only with tenant isolation, real ledgers, audit logs, and AI cost tracking — not static demo screens.

nodes.label: EduAI Engine (10 AI Features Layer)
nodes.rationale: Shared AI layer (admission counselor chatbot, digital twin, fee recovery, timetable generator, risk detection, meeting summary, command center, complaint resolver, career path engine, predictive analytics) built into every module so AI is a product differentiator rather than an add-on.

nodes.label: Student Digital Twin
nodes.rationale: Holistic AI-powered student profile (academic, behavioral, psychological, cognitive, career, risk factors) stored in student_profiles table; exists to enable early intervention, personalized learning paths, and career counseling from one data structure.

nodes.label: Glassmorphism Design System
nodes.rationale: Premium futuristic look (blur glass cards, cyber blue #0066FF, neon cyan #00F0FF gradients) chosen to make the product feel high-end; TravelOS guide adds the constraint to use glass only where text contrast stays readable and never white text on light glass.

nodes.label: Multi-Tenant SaaS Architecture
nodes.rationale: tenant_id (institution_id) on every business table with Supabase RLS filtering every query; super admin gets audited cross-tenant access. Chosen so one platform serves many institutions with white-label branding per tenant; tenant isolation bugs are treated as critical launch blockers in the risk register.

nodes.label: Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Realtime + Storage)
nodes.rationale: Chosen because it bundles PostgreSQL, built-in JWT auth, realtime subscriptions, and storage — 'perfect for SaaS'. Hard security rule repeated across docs: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY must exist only on the backend, never in the Vite frontend.

nodes.label: OpenRouter AI Integration (Multi-Model LLM Access)
nodes.rationale: Chosen for multi-model access (Claude, GPT-4, Gemini) with no vendor lock-in. The 2026 roadmap flags moving all AI calls server-side as launch-blocking because a VITE_-prefixed API key ships to every browser and can be drained; server-side metering also makes AI a billable Pro-plan feature.

nodes.label: AI Fee Recovery Assistant
nodes.rationale: Predicts payment delays from parent payment behavior, prioritizes families by risk score, forecasts monthly collection, and personalizes reminder tone — fee collection pain is treated as the workflow that sells the ERP to Indian schools.

nodes.label: AI Itinerary Builder (TravelOS Flagship)
nodes.rationale: Named the flagship TravelOS feature: drag-drop day planner plus Kashmir-specific route intelligence (weather-aware changes, road-closure fallback, gondola/permit/union constraints, crowd avoidance). Regional constraint knowledge is the moat over generic itinerary tools.
